footer.disclaimer: This site is not financial advice. It is a personal, informational tool only — always verify real terms with your bank, lender, or a qualified financial advisor before making any decision.
footer.privacy: All your data is stored locally in your browser. Nothing is sent to any server.

conditionsView.title: Start conditions
conditionsView.resetConfirmMessage: Every start condition — the apartment, income, savings, chosen plans — will be wiped with no way to undo it. This is not the same as “Redo the setup wizard”, which keeps your current data.

selectionSummary.noVariants: Nothing is shown on the chart yet — pick plans on the “Plans” page.

apartment.title: The apartment you want
apartment.instruction: How much does the apartment you're aiming to buy cost today? Its price grows over time on its own — you set the growth rate later.

appreciation.title: How fast apartments get pricier
appreciation.instruction: Average yearly price growth for apartments. The same rate indexes rent and the price of your current apartment when it sells. Use a long-run average, not a peak year.

existing.title: An apartment you own now
existing.instruction: Do you already own a flat you'd sell to help pay for the new one? If so, tell us what it's worth today. Leave it off if you're starting from nothing.

salary.title: Your monthly salary
salary.instruction: Your take-home income per month, before deciding how much of it goes to the flat. It builds up from month 0.

share.title: How much goes to the mortgage
share.instruction: What share of that salary are you willing to put toward rent, the loan, and saving? The rest stays your ordinary spending.

growth.title: Salary growth
growth.instruction: How fast your salary grows each year, and which month the raise lands in. Leave the rate at 0 if you'd rather plan on today's income.

savings.title: Money you've saved
savings.instruction: Everything you've already put aside outside an Otbasy deposit, as one sum — the head start you begin with.

otbasy.title: Otbasy deposit
otbasy.instruction: Do you have an Otbasy state-housing deposit? If so, its balance and accrued interest decide when the Otbasy loan becomes available.

rent.title: Rent you'd pay
rent.instruction: If a plan has you renting between selling and buying, this is the monthly rent. It's ignored by plans where you never rent.

plans.title: Which plans to compare
plans.instruction: Check the built-in plans you’d like to compare — and build your own if you like. You can change all of this later on the “Plans” page.

apartmentTab.title: Apartment to buy
apartmentTab.note: Where you live before buying — sell, rent, or live rent-free — is a per-plan decision, set in the “Plans” tab separately for each plan.
apartmentTab.existingNote: A start condition shared by every plan: the flat you'd sell to help fund the purchase. It appreciates at the same growth rate as the one you're buying. Turn it off and the plans that sell it go unavailable; the renting and rent-free plans become available instead.
apartmentTab.existingPriceHint: What it's worth now. Grows with the market up to the month a plan sells it. When it sells is a per-plan decision, set in the “Plans” tab.
apartmentTab.growthHint: Year over year: 12% = a year from now the price is 12% higher. Steps once every six months. The same rate indexes rent and the price of your apartment being sold. Use a long-run average, not a peak year: the threshold where conclusions flip is ≈9%, and in the 8–10% range the answer is decided by model details, not the market.

moneyTab.mortgageShareHint: The part of your salary that goes to rent, loan, and saving. Enter it as a percent or an exact amount — the two stay in sync. The rest is your ordinary spending and stays out of the model.
moneyTab.indexationHint: Salary steps up once a year, in the raise month below. Separate from the housing market: salary follows salaries, not apartment prices.
moneyTab.rentHint: Paid only while the apartment is sold and a new one is not yet bought. Indexed at the apartment growth rate — it is the price of the same market, and has no rate of its own.
moneyTab.existingNote: In month 0 everything merges into one deposit — the one each plan picks in the “Plans” tab, or the Otbasy account for the Otbasy plan. So only the total matters, not which bank holds it today. Only Otbasy stands apart: it has its own rate and its own CC.
moneyTab.savingsHint: Everything not on the Otbasy account, as one sum.
moneyTab.otbasyAccruedHint: How much the bank has credited so far. CC is computed from exactly this, so starting from zero would push the CC ≥ 5 threshold further out than it really is. Not added to the money — it is already inside the balance.
moneyTab.otbasyMonthsHint: Has no effect yet — recorded for later, against Otbasy's minimum saving term.

depositsTab.builtInNote: Preset deposits. They cannot be changed or removed here.
depositsTab.ownEmpty: Nothing yet. Add a deposit if you have a bank offer not listed above — and pick it on a plan in the “Plans” tab.
depositsTab.removeTitleDisabled: Cannot remove: a plan stores its money in it
depositsTab.payoutHint: 1 = withdraw any time, lose nothing. More — interest is forfeited on early withdrawal.

loansTab.builtInNote: Preset credits. They cannot be changed or removed here.
loansTab.ownEmpty: Nothing yet. Add a credit if you have a bank offer not listed above — and pick it in the “Plans” tab.
loansTab.removeTitleDisabled: Cannot remove: this credit is picked on a plan

plansTab.builtInNote: Preset plans. They cannot be changed or removed. Check which ones to show on the chart and in the summary.
plansTab.ownEmpty: Nothing yet. Build a plan out of a few decisions — e.g. “Halyk, but with a 50% down payment” — or let “Build best plans for me” do it.
plansTab.removeConfirmMessage: “{name}” will be removed with no way to undo it. If it's currently shown on the chart, it'll disappear from there too.
plansTab.saveMonthsHint: Empty = wait exactly as long as Otbasy does.
plansTab.saleMonthHint: The month the apartment you own now sells. You rent from here until you buy.
plansTab.depositHint: Where this plan stores money: today's savings, sale proceeds, and monthly contributions. Add your own in the “Deposits” tab.
plansTab.showMax: Chart shows at most {max} — unpick another plan first
plansTab.showNeedsApartment: Needs an apartment you own now — turn it on in “Start conditions”
plansTab.showNeedsNoApartment: For when you own no apartment — turn ownership off in “Start conditions”

runControls.horizonHint: A ceiling only: the calculation still cuts off once the last plan clears its debt. Worth raising if someone doesn't finish paying it off in time.

summaryTable.title: Comparing the options
summaryTable.subtitle: Target loan {loan} ₸ · compared over {months} months (up to the month the last option clears its debt)
summaryTable.dropped: Not counted: {list}. This plan waits exactly as long as Otbasy, and Otbasy never reaches a purchase within the horizon — so there is nothing to wait for. Set a saving window manually or raise the horizon.

scheduleTable.title: Month by month
scheduleTable.note: The purchase row is highlighted. Income is shown in the month interest is paid out, not accrued. Columns that are empty for this option are hidden.

netWorthChart.title: Net worth over time
netWorthChart.subtitle: Apartment + deposits − debt. Continuous across the sale and purchase months. The chart cuts off at month {month} — that's where the last option clears its debt, and there is nothing further to compare.
netWorthChart.empty: No plan is selected. Check plans above the chart or in the “Plans” tab.
netWorthChart.ariaLabel: Net worth by option over time

format.payoutMonthly: paid out every month, withdraw and lose nothing

repayLabels.lump: save up and clear at once

name.title: Name
name.instruction: What should this plan be called? It appears under this name on the chart and in the summary.

loan.title: Which loan
loan.instruction: How to pay for the flat: a bank mortgage, the Otbasy state-housing loan, or no loan — all cash.

buyWhen.title: When to buy
buyWhen.instruction: The moment this plan goes ahead and buys.

borrow.title: How much to borrow
borrow.instruction: How much of the price to put on the loan, and how much to pay upfront.

repay.title: How to repay
repay.instruction: What to do with spare cash: repay the loan early, or let it compound on a deposit.

situation.title: Current housing
situation.instruction: Where you live until you buy the new flat — and whether you sell the one you have.

deposit.title: Deposit for savings
deposit.instruction: Where this plan stores money until it buys. Add your own in the “Deposits” tab.

term.title: Loan term
term.instruction: A longer term means a smaller monthly payment; the shortest term you can afford means a bigger payment but a faster finish.

loan.none: Save up and buy with cash — no interest.
loan.otbasy: State programme: a lower rate, but you must save up and wait for its gates.

buyWhen.asap: Buy the first month you can afford it.
buyWhen.afterMonths: Wait a set number of months, saving first.
buyWhen.otbasyGates: Wait for Otbasy’s 50% balance and score gates.

borrow.min: Pay as much upfront as possible, borrow only the rest.

repay.monthly: Pour spare cash into the loan every month.
repay.lump: Save on a deposit and clear the loan in one hit.
repay.never: Pay only the schedule; the cash compounds on a deposit.

situation.selling: Sell the flat I own now, then rent.
situation.free: Live for free, nothing to sell.
situation.renting: Rent a place from the very start.

term.max: The longest term the bank allows — the smallest monthly payment.
term.shortest: The shortest term you can still afford — a bigger payment, paid off sooner.

bestPlans.title: Best plans
bestPlans.note: Try every plan your conditions allow and keep the winner of each category. You can disable, copy, or recalculate them — but not edit or delete.
bestPlans.noneFit: No plan managed to settle within your time budget. Try raising the maximum time and building again.

generatorDialog.title: Before we search
generatorDialog.instruction: A couple of facts we can't guess — they shape every plan we'll try.

sell.label: Sell it
sell.desc: Put the proceeds toward the new flat, then rent until you buy.

keep.label: Keep it
keep.desc: Don't sell — choose below where you'll live meanwhile.

generatorDialog.saleMonthHint: We start here and try selling every couple of months from this point.
generatorDialog.housingTitle: Where you'll live until you buy

free.label: Live for free
free.desc: No rent to pay until the new flat is yours.

renting.label: Rent
renting.desc: Pay rent from the start until you buy.

generatorDialog.maxYearsHint: A plan must own the flat with no debt left within this time, or it's dropped.

planDetails.title: Plan details
planDetails.notCleared: Loan not cleared within the horizon
